derivations.derivation_title: Centroid ȳ of a trapezoid (parallel sides a and b, height h)
derivations.derivation_stub: Derive the vertical centroid ȳ (measured from the base at y=0 where base width = a) of a trapezoid with parallel side widths a (at y=0) and b (at y=h) and height h: $$\bar{y}=\frac{h(a+2b)}{3(a+b)}$$
steps: Geometry / variable definition: place trapezoid so the lower base (width = a) is at y=0 and the upper base (width = b) is at y=h. The width varies linearly with y. Define the linear width function: $$w(y)=a+ky,\quad k=\frac{b-a}{h},\quad 0\le y\le h.$$
Area (denominator) via integration: $$A=\int_0^h w(y)\,dy=\int_0^h\big(a+ky\big)\,dy=ah+\tfrac12kh^2.$$ Substitute $k=(b-a)/h$ to get the familiar trapezoid area: $$A=\frac{(a+b)}{2}\,h.$$

First moment about base (numerator) via integration: $$M_y=\int_0^h y\,w(y)\,dy=\int_0^h\big(ay+ky^2\big)\,dy=\frac{a h^2}{2}+\frac{k h^3}{3}.$$ Substitute $k=(b-a)/h$ to simplify: $$M_y=\frac{a h^2}{2}+\frac{(b-a)h^2}{3}=h^2\Big(\frac{a}{2}+\frac{b-a}{3}\Big)=\frac{h^2(a+2b)}{6}.$$
Divide first moment by area to obtain centroid coordinate: $$\bar{y}=\frac{M_y}{A}=\frac{\dfrac{h^2(a+2b)}{6}}{\dfrac{(a+b)h}{2}}=\frac{h(a+2b)}{3(a+b)}.$$

Check special cases (consistency): (i) If a=b (rectangle) then $$\bar{y}=\dfrac{h( a+2a)}{3(2a)}=\dfrac{h}{2}$$ as expected. (ii) If b=0 (triangle with base a at bottom) then $$\bar{y}=\dfrac{h(a+0)}{3(a+0)}=\dfrac{h}{3}$$ as expected.
Completeness: derivation is complete (all integration and algebraic substitution steps shown).

derivations.derivation_stub: Derive the area of a trapezoid by integrating the linear width: $$A=\int_0^h w(y)\,dy=\frac{(a+b)}{2}h$$
steps: Start from the linear width function used in the main derivation: $$w(y)=a+\frac{(b-a)}{h}y.$$
Purpose: used as the denominator (total area) in the centroid calculation.

derivations.derivation_title: Centroid limits: triangle and rectangle as special cases
derivations.derivation_stub: Show that the trapezoid centroid formula reduces to known results for a triangle (b=0) and a rectangle (a=b): $$\bar{y}_{triangle}=\frac{h}{3},\quad\bar{y}_{rectangle}=\frac{h}{2}$$
steps: Purpose: validates the main derivation by recovering canonical centroid values.

derivations.derivation_title: Linear width expression for trapezoidal cross-sections
derivations.derivation_stub: Express width as a linear function of vertical coordinate y between two parallel sides: $$w(y)=a+\frac{(b-a)}{h}y$$
steps: Assume linear interpolation of widths between y=0 (width a) and y=h (width b). The slope k is $$k=\frac{b-a}{h}.$$
Purpose: provides the integrand used in both area and first moment integrals in the main derivation.
